hero.sub: Real-time transcription and structured summaries for lectures, meetings, and everyday conversations — processed entirely on-device.

footer.tagline: Lecture-notes app for students and researchers. Made in Tokyo. 100% on-device.

stt.body: Whisper runs on your Mac. No upload, no waiting. Captions appear live, second-by-second, with timestamps.

privacy.body: Whisper + Llama models run locally. No cloud transcription, no recording uploaded, no third-party data processor.

notes.headlineBefore: Not a wall of text — a
notes.body: Llama 3.2 extracts sections, key terms, and bullets. Formatted as Markdown, ready to read or edit.

export.body: Markdown export means your notes live where you live. Obsidian, Notion, plain folder, anywhere your editor reads .md.

features.marginalia: No upload. No cloud. No data processor — your lecture, your laptop, your notes.

privacyEmphasis.statSub: of audio stays on your Mac. Not 99.9% — actually all of it.
privacyEmphasis.item1Body: Whisper and Llama 3.2 both run on-device.
privacyEmphasis.item2Body: Lisna doesn't ping our servers with usage data. Plausible on website only (anonymous, no cookies).
privacyEmphasis.item3Body: Markdown on your Mac. Sync to Obsidian / iCloud / Dropbox — your choice.
privacyEmphasis.item4Body: Email + signin metadata. No transcription content ever touches our database.

pricingSection.heading: Free during alpha.
pricingSection.sub: Pay only when alpha ends — at fair, predictable pricing.

faq.q1: Why is Lisna macOS-only at launch?
faq.a1: Our on-device LLM uses Apple's Metal GPU APIs. Windows / Linux support is on the roadmap after we validate the macOS alpha.
faq.q2: What languages does the transcription support?
faq.a2: Japanese, English, and Korean at launch. Whisper supports 90+ languages; we expand as the model evolves.
faq.a3: Yes. Audio is transcribed locally; notes are saved on your Mac. Lisna's servers never see your audio or note content.
faq.q4: What happens to my data if I uninstall?
faq.a4: Your notes stay where they are on disk (Markdown files). The app removal does not delete content. Your account record on Lisna's server can be deleted via Discord support.
faq.q5: How do I export to Obsidian?
faq.a5: Set your Obsidian vault path in Settings. New notes save into the vault directly. Existing notes can be moved or symlinked.
faq.a6: Yes, post-macOS-alpha. Timeline depends on alpha feedback and model performance on non-Apple-Silicon hardware.

ctaStrip.sub: Free during alpha. Sign in inside the app on first launch.
ctaStrip.bullet2: Your notes are always yours to keep

pricingPage.heading: Pricing
pricingPage.intro: Lisna is in alpha and free for early users. The Pro tier turns on after alpha concludes.
pricingPage.v2Sub: Pay only when alpha ends — at fair, predictable pricing.
pricingPage.v1Body: The Chrome extension version of Lisna remains available at the existing price. It uses cloud transcription and is being maintained alongside v2.

comparePage.title: Lisna vs cloud-based tools
comparePage.intro: What you get when transcription, structuring, and storage all run on your Mac.
comparePage.row3: No data sent to LLM provider
comparePage.whyP1: Cloud transcription is fast to build but loud about your data. Audio is uploaded to a vendor, transcribed on their GPUs, structured by their LLM, and stored on their servers. For students and researchers handling lectures, drafts, and unpublished ideas, that flow is wrong.
comparePage.whyP2: Lisna inverts it. Whisper runs on your Mac's Neural Engine. Llama 3.2 runs in your Mac's RAM. Notes write to your filesystem in Markdown — sync them with Obsidian or iCloud or no one if you prefer.
comparePage.whyP3: This means Lisna is slower on first launch (model downloads). It means we can't ship feature parity with cloud-only tools on day one. We think the trade is worth it.
